Section2.title: Three audiences benefit most from V1-A beta.
Section2.subtitle: ACM isn't built for everyone. Here's who we're watching for.

personaB.label: PLATFORM TEAM
personaB.title: Platform teams evaluating build vs buy
personaB.desc: Multi-tenant + approval governance + self-deployable. No data lock-in.

personaC.label: CREATOR / EDUCATOR
personaC.title: AI educators & creators
personaC.desc: Workflow visualization + call receipts + tier quotas. Demo / teach without burning your wallet.

personaD.label: INDIE OPERATOR
personaD.title: Indie operators on the road
personaD.desc: One-click deploy + heartbeat monitoring + error retry. Remove ops from your headspace.

Section3.title: Three-layer backbone + tool governance.
Section3.subtitle: ACM doesn't write your agents. ACM is the 'management' layer once they're running.
Section3.cap1Desc: DAG topology + completion hook + edge conditions. e.g. customer query → risk approval → ticket creation, declaratively composed.
Section3.cap2Desc: tool-proxy interception + approval gate + tier quotas. High-risk actions go through human approval, low-risk pass through.
Section3.cap3Desc: heartbeat scheduler + cron. Run daily report agent at 9am; auto-alert on failure.
Section3.mcpHeadline: 5 official MCP servers preinstalled. Half require approval by default.
Section3.mcpFootnote: Install any npm/uv MCP server via mcp_installer with one click. Based on the official ModelContextProtocol ecosystem (1300+ third-party servers available).

Section4.title: Audit-based. No fairy tales.
Section4.subtitle: ✅ Based on real codebase audit (47 modules). Every line maps to git log.

Section5.title: Why I built ACM
Section5.line1: I'm the author of ACM. Former game PM, now a digital nomad —
Section5.line2: building from the road, running a one-person company.
Section5.line3: I believe AI shouldn't replace life; it should give you more of it.
Section5.line4: ACM is the tool I built for that path.

Section6.title: 5 common questions
Section6.subtitle: PO selected.
Section6.numberingNote: Numbering preserves PO Q4 original IDs (F5/F6/F7 not selected — decision: ship to docs, not landing).

F1.q: Can I self-deploy ACM? Will my data stay on my server?
F1.a: Yes. ACM follows an Open Core model — the repo is fully OSS on GitHub, self-hosting is free, you control your data. Our Hosted service (agents.ngzr.net) charges at cost (server + LLM tokens). You only pay for compute, never for the software itself.

F2.q: How is ACM different from LangChain / CrewAI / n8n / Coze?
F2.a: LangChain / CrewAI are SDKs for individual developers writing agent code. ACM is the ops layer for people who already have agents — approvals, tool calls, heartbeats, quotas, team workspace. n8n / Coze are low-code platforms for non-engineers. ACM assumes you can write agents and gives you the engineer-grade governance tooling. In short: they write agents, ACM manages them.

F3.q: Can ACM really run a '24h AI company'? How far along is it?
F3.a: About 70% done. The backend is in place: PM Agent auto-creates workflows, 4-role virtual team (manager / executor / reflector / notetaker), multi-agent collaboration patterns, heartbeat scheduling, tool governance, 2 business templates (rental + travel ops). The remaining 30%: UX polish, quota enforcement, automatic error recovery, more templates. V1-A beta is where you come in — use it, roast it, help us grow the last 30%.

F4.q: Pricing model? Is V1-A free during beta?
F4.a: V1-A beta is free (self-hosting is free forever). The Hosted service later passes through cost: pay for server time + LLM tokens (small ops markup). The software itself is never charged for.

F8.q: Can I contribute code / fork / build derivatives?
F8.a: Yes. Repo will be OSS (during V1-A). Forks / derivatives fully allowed. Community PRs welcome — especially business domain templates (e-commerce, customer service, content production — your expertise areas).

ApplyPage.title: Get Beta Access
ApplyPage.subtitle: Tell us what you want to build with ACM. We'll review and send an invite code to your inbox.
ApplyPage.intentLabel: What are you thinking of using ACM for?
ApplyPage.intentPlaceholder: Building an internal AI ops platform for 5-person team / Embedding agents into my indie SaaS / Teaching AI engineering and need a demo backend / ...
ApplyPage.intentHelper: This is the core signal we use to review — a sentence about your scenario; the more specific, the better.
ApplyPage.errorBody: Network issue or service busy. Please try again shortly.
ApplyPage.thankYouBody: We'll review shortly. If approved, an invite code will land in your inbox.
ApplyPage.privacyNote: Your email is used only for invite code + critical updates. No marketing, no third-party sharing.
